change: Reduce the rate-limit cooldown fallback from 3600s to 300s (5 min) at line 489. The 1-hour default is too aggressive — it blocks all providers for the entire hour even if the rate limit resets sooner. Also, skip the cooldown entirely if the failure was auth-related (any_rate_limited would be False for auth, but add an explicit guard). Additionally, log the cooldown duration to memory_log.jsonl so we can observe how often and how long cooldowns fire.
reason: When the system resumes after auth is fixed, the first rate limit hit will sleep for 1 hour (3600s fallback). This wastes an entire hour per rate-limit event when 5 minutes is typically sufficient. The fix improves cycle throughput by 12x on rate-limit events.

now: Reduce rate-limit cooldown fallback from 3600s to 300s in agent.py
Add cooldown logging to memory_log.jsonl for observability
Verify auth guard is working correctly with current failure history
next: Refactor _run_once() stream parsing — extract _spawn_cli helper already exists but rate-limit retry loop duplicates the full spawn logic
Add provider health caching to skip known-dead providers within same cycle
Improve fitness scoring to weight auth failures at 0.0 instead of 0.25
Add structured failure taxonomy beyond auth/none (timeout, syntax, import, runtime)
later: Adaptive cooldown based on historical rate-limit patterns
Long-horizon mutation campaigns with rollback checkpoints
Cross-session learning transfer via structured memory compression

diagnosis.weakest_module: agent.py — 3600s rate-limit cooldown fallback is too aggressive; blocks all progress for 1 hour on any rate limit event
diagnosis.root_cause: Auth infrastructure issue (CLI not logged in), not code. Code guards are correct. Next code improvement targets throughput recovery when auth is restored.
